newsSinceJun6: No new material news since June 6. Stock remains a cash merger-arb stub: Baker Hughes is acquiring Chart for $210/share cash ($13.6B EV); shareholders approved Oct 2025, HSR waiting period expired Nov 6, 2025, and the EU Commission has a June 26, 2026 Phase 1 decision deadline. Shares trade ~$207, a ~1.4% spread to deal price, with deal close targeted mid-2026 (outside date July 28, 2026).

sources.label: MLex - Baker Hughes seeks EU clearance for $13.6bn Chart deal (EC deadline June 26)
Baker Hughes - to Acquire Chart Industries ($210/share cash)
Quiver Quant - Chart shareholders approve acquisition by Baker Hughes
Yahoo Finance - GTLS quote (price pinned near $207)

verdicts.rankRationale: Already last-ranked on this strict latent-node screen and nothing new since June 6; as a $210 all-cash Baker Hughes deal stub with only ~1.4% spread left and an EC decision due June 26, the latent AI-cooling optionality cannot accrue to this equity, so the bottom rank with mild score decay is correct.
verdicts.bull: If the EU were to block or force a renegotiation (outside date July 28, 2026), GTLS reverts to a standalone cryogenic/gas-handling franchise with genuine data-center cooling and LNG optionality at a sub-$10B cap.
verdicts.neutral: Base case is EU Phase 1 clearance by June 26 and a mid-2026 close at $210; the stock is a low-volatility arb position earning ~1.4% to deal price, with zero expression of the AI-bottleneck thesis.
verdicts.bear: An EU Phase 2 referral or remedy fight extends the timeline and widens the spread, while as a near-certain acquisition target the name occupies a slot on this list that delivers no asymmetric alpha either way.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: European Commission Phase 1 decision on the Baker Hughes acquisition, deadline June 26, 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deal closes as expected at $210, capping upside at ~1.4% and removing the equity from the investable universe mid-2026.
